title: Solah somvar vrat katha
intro: Read Shri Solah Somvar Vrat Katha in English and seek the divine blessings of Lord Shiva for wish fulfillment and spiritual growth.
description:
Solah Somvar Vrat Katha

Once, Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati arrived in the city of Amravati.
They stayed in a Shiva temple to rest during their world tour.
One day, Parvati expressed a desire to play a game of dice (Chausar).

During the game, the temple priest arrived at the scene.
Parvati asked the priest to predict the winner of the game.
The priest hastily predicted that Lord Shiva would win.
However, Parvati won the game, and the priest's error angered her.
She cursed the priest, causing him to suffer from leprosy.

The priest suffered greatly due to the painful disease.
Later, some Apsaras (celestial beings) visited the temple.
Seeing the priest's suffering, they felt deep pity for him.
They advised him to observe the 'Solah Somvar Vrat' (16 Mondays Fast).

The ritual: Take half a seer of clean wheat flour on a Monday.
Make three parts (baatis) from the dough.
Offer prayers to Lord Shiva with ghee, jaggery, and incense.
Offer one part to Shiva, share one as Prasad, and eat the third.
On the 17th Monday, make 'churma' from the flour and share it.
The priest followed these instructions and was soon cured.

When Shiva and Parvati returned, they were surprised to see him healthy.
Upon learning the power of the fast, Parvati performed it for Kartikeya.
Kartikeya then performed the fast to reunite with his friend.
His friend went to another kingdom and married a princess.

The princess performed the fast to be blessed with a son.
Her son performed it later to gain the right to the throne.
He became a King, but a misunderstanding occurred during a ritual.
The Queen asked her maids to prepare the offering, which displeased Shiva.
A divine voice forced the King to abandon his innocent Queen.

The Queen wandered in exile, facing misery wherever she went.
Everything she touched seemed to spoil or rot.
Finally, she sought refuge in a sage's ashram.
The sage advised her to observe the Solah Somvar Vrat with devotion.

She performed the fast, and the King dreamt of her whereabouts.
He sent messengers who found her at the sage's ashram.
The King realized the truth, apologized, and brought her home.
They were reunited, ruled justly, and lived happily ever after.
They eventually attained liberation and went to Shiva's abode.

॥ Glory to Lord Shiva ॥

faq.q1: When should one start the Solah Somvar Vrat?
faq.a1: Starting on a Monday in the month of Shravan is considered most auspicious, but you can begin on any Monday of the year.
faq.q2: How long does the Vrat last?
faq.a2: As the name suggests, this Vrat is observed for sixteen consecutive Mondays.
faq.q3: What are the main requirements for the Puja?
faq.a3: You will need half a seer (approx. 500g) of wheat flour, ghee, jaggery, sandalwood, rice grains, Belpatra (Bael leaves), and flowers.
faq.q4: When and how is the Udyapan (conclusion) performed?
faq.a4: The Udyapan is performed on the seventeenth Monday. A sweet dish called 'Churma' is prepared as an offering to Lord Shiva, and food is distributed to the needy or Brahmins.
faq.q5: What are the benefits of this Vrat?
faq.a5: Observing this fast brings the blessings of Lord Shiva, helps overcome suffering, fulfills heartfelt wishes, and attracts peace and prosperity into one's life.
